behavior_in_task_description.reason: Tests check many specifics not mentioned in instruction.md: test_global_json_valid checks for 'tools', 'dotnet', 'msbuild-sdks' keys in global.json; test_nuget_config_valid checks root tag is 'configuration'; test_build_scripts_executable checks for shebang in build.sh; test_solution_file_exists checks for 'Microsoft Visual Studio Solution File' string; test_editorconfig_exists checks 'root = true'; test_git_repo_clean requires all changes committed; test_copilot_instructions_valid checks '---'

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction.md gives extremely specific implementation details: 'use File.WriteAllText to write these files as triple-quoted raw string literals containing XML with <Project> as the root element', 'Must set $ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"', 'Must include [CmdletBinding()] attribute', 'Must start with YAML frontmatter (--- at the first line)', 'Must include the text "ALWAYS use this skill"'. These are exact implementation directives, not descriptions of broken behavior.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests like test_powershell_script_valid assert on specific implementation details: script must start with '#' or '<#', must contain 'param(' literal, must contain 'ValidateSet', must contain 'ErrorActionPreference'. test_copilot_instructions_valid requires '---' in first 10 chars. test_skill_md_yaml_frontmatter checks for exact frontmatter structure. A correct alternative (e.g., using a different error handling mechanism or different comment style) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Nearly all tests are text-matching: checking file contents with string searches like 'assert "param(" in content', 'assert "ValidateSet" in content', 'assert "---" in content[:10]', 'assert "class" in src'. The only execution-based tests are git commands (test_git_repo_clean, test_git_no_merge_conflicts) which test repo state, not the agent's code behavior. No test actually runs the PowerShell script, builds the C# code, or tests MSBuild isolation behavior.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests like test_powershell_script_valid require the file to exist and contain specific content (param block, ValidateSet, etc.) — a stub empty file or pass-through wouldn't satisfy these. test_csharp_syntax_valid requires balanced braces/parens. These require non-trivial file creation/modification.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction specifies MSBuild isolation via creating empty Directory.Build.props/targets in the test directory root with File.WriteAllText, but no test verifies this behavior was actually implemented in BaseBuildTest.cs (test_not_stub only checks for >=3 File.* operations which likely already pass). The instruction mentions 'dotnet test' automation workflow but no test verifies the script actually works.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-organized with descriptive names (test_csharp_syntax_valid, test_powershell_script_valid, etc.), docstrings explaining purpose, and comments grouping them into categories ([static] pass_to_pass, [repo_tests] pass_to_pass).

structured_data_schema.reason: The task produces documentation files, scripts, and C# code modifications — not structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, variable names, and commands appear consistent between instruction.md and tests. Category names (Build, WindowsTemplates, macOSTemplates, Blazor, MultiProject, Samples, AOT, RunOnAndroid, RunOniOS) match across all files.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction.md names the exact file to modify (BaseBuildTest.cs), the exact method (SetUpNuGetPackages), and gives precise implementation: 'use File.WriteAllText to write these files as triple-quoted raw string literals containing XML with <Project> as the root element'. This goes well beyond describing what's broken.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch creates new files and makes localized additions to existing config files (copilot-instructions.md, integration-tests.instructions.md). The diffs are scoped additions, not full-file rewrites.
